signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike probability priced for the May 27 meeting. RBNZ nominal rate at 2.25% vs Fed 3.75% maintains a -150bp carry deficit, but a hawkish delivery or signal tomorrow triggers elevated short-squeeze risk for crowded speculative shorts. NZD/USD at 0.5839 testing recent lows while CESI remains neutral at -2.

signals.title: AUD/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + CPI Focus
signals.text: AUD/NZD G8 spread at 0.945% as AUD outperforms (+0.256%) and NZD lags (-0.689%) across G8 peers. AUD/USD at 0.7173 remains supported by over CHF and -84bp OIS premium over USD. Upcoming Australia CPI data determines if the RBA's 4.35% hiking stance maintains this outperformance. Retail sentiment at 92% short AUD/NZD signals extreme contrarian reversal risk if CPI surprises to the upside.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.29 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Hormuz firefight headlines reviving USD demand. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 (long% 31.2%) remains crowded. 160.00 remains the key level for BoJ verbal warnings or intervention. 25d RR at -1.41 (moderate put skew) confirms the options market is paying for downside protection against intervention despite the carry-driven spot bid.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 27.5% vs HV30 at 8.3% generates a +19.2% Vol Risk Premium, signaling extreme institutional hedging demand ahead of CPI. Retail sentiment is 74% short, diverging from COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long). Institutional positioning and options pricing suggest a breakout bias, while retail fades the trend. Watch 0.7200 resistance for a squeeze higher.
